query: Zelenskyy Ukraine Statehood Day 2026 decree award recipients culture science education sports list
a_issues: Content A includes one highly relevant official President of Ukraine result mentioning the needed categories (culture, publishing, science, education), but its snippet is truncated and does not expose recipient names or a list. Other results are partly off-target: the Interfax item is about decrees 67-69/2026 and foreign/military awardees rather than the Statehood Day decree focused on culture/science/education/sports; the Mezha result is also broad ceremony coverage, not the recipient list sought. One result (Intent) gives only a single regional example. It also omits sports in the presidential snippet shown.
b_issues: Content B still does not provide the actual recipient list in-snippet. The presidential result is also truncated, though it is more on-target and explicitly matches the needed categories better than A. Some results are potentially distracting or separate matters: the Ukrinform scholarships item is about decrees 495/2026 and 496/2026, not the Statehood Day honors decree; the Interfax item again concerns decrees 67-69/2026; the UNN result about the 'National Legend of Ukraine' award is likely a different award set than the queried Statehood Day decree. The NAS result suggests decree 604/2026 but does not expose names in the snippet.
reasoning: B better enables a correct answer because it contains the most directly relevant official source snippet: the President of Ukraine page explicitly says, 'Volodymyr Zelenskyy Presented State Awards and Conferred Honorary Titles on Representatives of Culture, Publishing, Science, Education, and ...', which aligns closely with the query and strongly points to the correct decree/event. B also includes a NAS page explicitly tying this to Decree No. 604/2026 on July 15, 2026, which matches the anchor. A has a similar official presidential result, but its snippet is slightly less complete for the queried sectors and the rest of A leans more toward unrelated military/foreign-partner decrees and ceremony coverage. Neither set fully exposes the requested recipient list from snippets alone, so both are imperfect; however, B is more accurately centered on the specific Statehood Day decree and categories asked about.
